title: compare_combat_trace --align move pairs by record index but native emits 1 rec/frame vs ares ~2 rec/tick — ~2x sim-tick skew inflates+misattributes combat divergences
repro: dam_combat_guard6 both-sides: --align move gives 666 guards.actiontype divergences / 18657 total; true sim-tick alignment gives 249 (-63%) / 9327 (-50%) AND INVERTS FID-0054 (native perceives Bond LATER +108 vs stock +76 ticks, not earlier). ares emits ~2 records/advancing-global-tick (intra-frame AI + empty-roster samples) vs native 1/game-frame.

history.note: Discovered by FID-0054 ACT re-analysis (§11). This is the LINCHPIN instrumentation defect: --align move systematically skews timelines ~2x in sim-tick space, so a large fraction of combat-oracle guard divergences are alignment artifacts, not port defects. FIX: --align tick mode (agent prototyped, reverted — naive version added a 'present' artifact 3492 hits because ares interleaves full/empty-roster records; needs careful design handling the interleave). BLOCKS trustworthy combat-parity ACT: FID-0054 (perception, now known-inverted), FID-0011/0012/0013 (chrTickBeams/stan LOS/guard-coupling — all seeded from --align move, must be RE-VALIDATED under sim-tick alignment before any ACT). Residual real divergence after alignment fix = systemic PRNG call-count desync (rng_seed, §5/§8) + tiny position drift.
